label: Distributed data pipelines & streaming

node.label: Distributed data pipelines & streaming
node.definition: Distributed data pipelines ingest, transform, validate, shuffle, stream, cache, and feed training or inference workloads at sustained throughput.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Distributed data pipelines & streaming is modeled as depending on Distributed systems & networking, Databases & information retrieval and feeding downstream nodes such as Workflow, cluster & job orchestration, Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps.
examples: atlas downstream link to Workflow, cluster & job orchestration
atlas downstream link to Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps
limitations: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Distributed systems & networking, Databases & information retrieval.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Distributed data pipelines & streaming has an `operationalizes` dependency on Distributed systems & networking because Distributed systems & networking is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

In this atlas graph, Distributed data pipelines & streaming has an `operationalizes` dependency on Data collection, curation & filtering because Data collection, curation & filtering is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

In this atlas graph, Distributed data pipelines & streaming has an `operationalizes` dependency on Multimodal, simulated & streaming data because Multimodal, simulated & streaming data is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.
